scope: Coheronia v0.2 increment: tool-tier progression, food loop, light occlusion, threat persistence.
summary: Extends the signed v0.1 slice along the HANDOFF's own v0.2 candidates. Ore now requires a tier-2 pick forged at the Town Hall (3 wood + 5 stone from the stockpile, recipe basic_pick_upgrade); higher tiers mine ~50% faster per tier. Berry bushes spawn on the surface (separate RNG stream keeps same-seed terrain identical) and drop food; settlers eat 2 food at each dawn and shortages raise a now food-aware scarcity_penalty. Every blocks_light tile carries an occluder polygon and torch lights cast shadows, so walls block light and caves are dark. Saves are version 0.2 with threat persistence ({x,y,hp}); v0.1 saves still load.
acceptance: Ore exists and is NOT mineable at tier 1; forging at the hall consumes stockpile and sets tier 2
Tier 2 mines dirt in 14 frames vs 21 at tier 1; ore mineable after forge (64 frames)
Berry bush mined for 2 food; food depositable; dawn consumption eats food 2->0 with log feedback
TileSet has an occlusion layer, blocks_light tiles have occluders, torch lights shadow-enabled
Save/load round trip restores live threats and tool tier alongside all v0.1 state
All 23 pre-existing checks unchanged (mining frames identical 21/33/66); suite total 34/34, exit 0
Hall-framed night screenshot reviewed: torch glow with shadows, dark occluded terrain, restored threat visible, HUD shows Pick tier 2 and food events
validation: COHERONIA_SMOKE=1 headless run -> SMOKE RESULT: PASS (34/34), exit 0
decisions: Ore raised to required_tool_tier 2 in blocks.json to make the existing forge recipe a real progression gate (start tier 1 keeps the MVP acceptance 'can mine dirt/stone/wood' intact)
Berry bushes generated on a separate RNG stream (seed+7) so pre-v0.2 worlds regenerate byte-identical terrain and trees
Food shortage feeds scarcity_penalty rather than dealing direct damage, keeping pressure systemic per the C/L/R design
Occlusion uses TileData occluder polygons with a has_method fallback for older TileData APIs
Save version bumped to 0.2 with explicit 0.1 acceptance instead of rejecting old saves
risks: Food pacing untested by human play (2/dawn vs ~0.07 bushes/column, no regrowth)
Shadow-casting lights unmeasured at extreme torch counts (expected fine at this scale)
next_action: Operator playthrough of v0.2, then choose v0.3 scope from HANDOFF candidates.
